mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year rolling window, or negative UNRATE changes produce positive SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive monetary policy that raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice risk.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, signaling recession risk that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month AND HOUST falls below 2000 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2120 thousand units as of Aug 2011
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling weakening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side constraints that reduce corporate profitability and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth < 0.5%, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50 thousand new jobs per month, indicating labor market weakness that reduces consumer income growth and aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134966 thousand as of Sept 2011
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth < 50k, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 4.0%) signals monetary policy tightening that erodes real returns on equities and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors rotate to fixed income.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 199.1 as of Aug 2011, FEDFUNDS at 4.59%
failure_condition: When both inflation and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 52% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.05 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as growth recession fears emerge.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11248.3 billion as of Q2 2011
failure_condition: When GDPC1 growth < 1.5%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 54% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1800 thousand units while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or declining, indicating consumer demand collapse that signals imminent recession and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1800 thousand units AND RSAFS month-over-month change <= 0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2120 thousand units as of Aug 2011
regime_hint: demand shock with housing market collapse
failure_condition: When both HOUST and RSAFS trigger conditions are met, SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 56% of instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month rolling window.
